Good question — this is exactly what examiners expect in viva 👇
Your current code works on **same machine**, but the question asks for **two different machines**.

Let’s convert your setup to **real distributed RMI (2 systems)**.

---

# 🧠 What changes in “2 different machines”?

Instead of:

```text
Client → localhost → Server
```

You will have:

```text
Client (Machine B) → IP address → Server (Machine A)
```

---

# 🖥️ System Setup

## 💻 Machine A (Server)

* Runs: Server + RMI Registry
* Has IP: example `192.168.1.10`

## 💻 Machine B (Client)

* Runs: Client
* Connects to Server using IP

---

# 🔥 Step-by-Step (VERY IMPORTANT)

---

## ✅ Step 1: Find Server IP (Machine A)

On Ubuntu:

```bash
ifconfig
```

or

```bash
ip a
```

👉 Example output:

```text
192.168.1.10
```

---

## ✅ Step 2: Modify Server Code (IMPORTANT CHANGE)

In `Server.java`, change:

```java
Naming.rebind("Server", obj);
```

👉 To:

```java
Naming.rebind("rmi://192.168.1.10/Server", obj);
```

(Replace with your actual IP)

---

## ✅ Step 3: Modify Client Code

In `Client.java`, change:

```java
Naming.lookup("rmi://localhost/Server");
```

👉 To:

```java
Naming.lookup("rmi://192.168.1.10/Server");
```

---

# ⚠️ MOST IMPORTANT LINE (Often asked in viva)

Add this in **Server.java before binding**:

```java
System.setProperty("java.rmi.server.hostname", "192.168.1.10");
```

---

## Final Server Code (important part)

```java
LocateRegistry.createRegistry(1099);
System.setProperty("java.rmi.server.hostname", "192.168.1.10");
Naming.rebind("rmi://192.168.1.10/Server", obj);
```

---

# 🚀 Execution Steps

---

## 🖥️ On Machine A (Server)

```bash
javac *.java
java Server
```

👉 Output:

```text
Server has started .
```

---

## 💻 On Machine B (Client)

👉 Copy ONLY these files:

* `Client.java`
* `ServerIntf.java`

Then:

```bash
javac *.java
java Client
```

---

# 🔐 Important Network Conditions

## ✅ Both machines must be:

* On same WiFi / LAN
* Example: College lab network

---

## ❌ If not working → check firewall

On Ubuntu:

```bash
sudo ufw allow 1099
```

---

# ⚠️ Common Errors + Fix

## ❌ `Connection refused`

✔ Server not running
✔ Wrong IP

---

## ❌ `UnknownHostException`

✔ Wrong IP format

---

## ❌ `NotBoundException`

✔ Name mismatch (`Server`)

---

## ❌ Timeout error

✔ Network issue
✔ Firewall blocking

---

# 🧠 Viva Explanation (VERY IMPORTANT)

If examiner asks:

👉 “How is communication happening?”

You say:

> “Client looks up remote object using server IP and registry. Server exports object using RMI registry and client invokes remote methods over network.”

---

# 🎯 Summary

| Step | Action                    |
| ---- | ------------------------- |
| 1    | Get Server IP             |
| 2    | Replace localhost with IP |
| 3    | Set hostname in server    |
| 4    | Run server on Machine A   |
| 5    | Run client on Machine B   |

---

# 🚀 Pro Tip (to impress examiner)

Say this:

> “RMI uses stub internally and method calls are serialized and sent over TCP/IP.”

---

If you want, next I can:

* Simulate **exact lab scenario**
* Or give **viva questions + answers** for this experiment 👍
